extra.message: User-controlled registration_code is interpolated directly into a raw SQL query via f-string and sqlalchemy.sql.text(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to bypass registration code validation, extract data, or modify the database.

User-controlled search parameter from request.args.get('search') is interpolated into a raw SQL LIKE clause via f-string and sqlalchemy.sql.text(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to read any records or modify the database.

request.path is interpolated via f-string into a string passed to render_template_string(). An attacker can request a URL containing Jinja2 template syntax (e.g., /{{config}}) to achieve Server-Side Template Injection, potentially leading to remote code execution.

pickle.loads() is called on cookie data (preferences) from the user via base64 decode. Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution. This runs on every request (before_request handler).

urllib.request.urlopen() is called with an arbitrary user-supplied URL from the image upload form. An attacker can use file:// URIs to read local files (e.g., /etc/passwd) or internal network addresses for SSRF.
The POST /account route (update_account) is missing the @login_required decorator. An unauthenticated attacker can attempt to mutate account data via this endpoint.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The POST /darkmode route (toggle_darkmode) is missing the @login_required decorator. The route still reads and writes user preferences cookies.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The AccountForm includes an is_admin BooleanField, and the update_account route blindly merges all form fields into current_user.__dict__ via .update(). Any authenticated user can set is_admin=True to escalate privileges to admin.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The GET /accounts/<int:user_id>/notes route returns all notes (including private) for any user_id with no check that user_id matches current_user.id. Any logged-in user can view any other user's private notes.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The POST /notes/<int:note_id>/delete route deletes any note by ID without checking that note.user_id == current_user.id. Any logged-in user can delete any other user's notes by guessing or enumerating note IDs.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The note.text field is rendered with the Jinja2 |safe filter, disabling HTML escaping. Since note text is user-controlled via the CKEditor form and no server-side sanitization is performed, an attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the browsers of all users viewing public notes.

Note text is rendered via render_table() with safe_columns=['text'], which disables HTML escaping for the text column. User-controlled note content can contain malicious JavaScript executed in any viewer's browser.

Note text is rendered via render_table() with safe_columns=['text'], which disables HTML escaping for the text column. User-controlled note content can contain malicious JavaScript executed in any viewer's browser.
Flask app.secret_key is hardcoded as 'super secret key' in source code. This secret is used to sign session cookies and should be loaded from an environment variable or secure configuration.
A static registration code 'a36e990b-0024-4d55-b74a-f8d7528e1764' is hardcoded in the database seed script. This code grants access to user registration and is embedded in source code.
